title: Quadlet - GroupAdd does not work rootless
body:
### Issue Description

Hi there,

I am trying to set up my containers as Quadlets/systemd services. Many of them running rootless and with group permissions.

I am using Fedora 43 Server Edition, with podman 5.7.1

The commands work fine with `proton run` as in

```
podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups ubi8 id
```
And I can confirm the ids.

`uid=1001(rootless_usr) gid=1002(rootless_usr) groups=1002(rootless_usr),1001(dir)`

`uid=1001(rootless_usr) gid=1002(rootless_usr) groups=1002(rootless_usr),65534(nobody)`

The same does not work as Quadlet for rootless users. But it does work for root user.

```ini
#ubi8.container
[Container]
Exec=id
Image=ubi8
#PodmanArgs=--group-add keep-groups
GroupAdd=keep-groups
UserNS=keep-id
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

**as a rootless user:**

1. on terminal enter `id` > uid=1001 gid=1002 groups=1002,1001
2. `podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups ubi8 id` > uid=1001 gid=1002 groups=1002,65534(nobody)
3. Add the `ubi8.container` described above to ~/.config/containers/systemd/
4. systemctl --user daemon-reload
5. systemctl --user start ubi8
6. systemctl --user status ubi8 > `Jan 07 17:48:12 fedora_server systemd-ubi8[110952]: uid=1001(rootless_usr) gid=1002(rootless_usr) groups=1002(rootless_usr)`

*nobody is missing

**as root user**

1. on terminal enter `id` > uid=1000 gid=1000 groups=1000,1001
2. `podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups ubi8 id` > uid=1000 gid=1000 groups=1000,65534(nobody)
3. Add the `ubi8.container` described above to ~/.config/containers/systemd/
4. systemctl --user daemon-reload
5. systemctl --user start ubi8
6. systemctl --user status ubi8 > `Jan 07 17:44:11 fedora_server systemd-ubi8[110781]: uid=1000(root) gid=1000(root) groups=1000(root),65534(no `

### Describe the results you received

Group 1001 has access to the files I need. Their permission appears as `65534(nobody)` inside the container, and I can access them just fine rootless with:

podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups -v /mnt/dir:/dir:ro ubi8 ls -lah /dir

but I cannot access them with:

```ini
# ubi8.container
[Container]
Exec=ls -lah /dir
Image=ubi8
PodmanArgs=--group-add keep-groups
UserNS=keep-id
Volume=/mnt/dir:/dir:ro
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the Quadlet to behave similar to the `podman run` command when used rootless. And similar to the root Quadlet.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Fedora Linux 43 (Server Edition)

### Additional information

I also tried adding to the file ubi8.container

`PodmanArgs=--group-add keep-groups`

and 

`Annotation=run.oci.keep_original_groups=1`

Instead of GroupAdd. Without success.

comments.body:
I tried reproducing this issue, but it worked as you expected it to.

I did the following:
```
# Create the groups
$ sudo groupadd -g 1001 dir
$ sudo groupadd -g 1002 rootless_usr
# Create the user, assign default and additional group
$ sudo useradd -u 1001 -g 1002 -G 1001 rootless_usr
```

I then logged-in with the new user.

Checking that executing podman directly works as expected: 
```
$ podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups ubi8 id
uid=1001(rootless_usr) gid=1002(rootless_usr) groups=1002(rootless_usr),65534(nobody)
```

I then created the `ubi.container` file
```
$ cat ~/.config/containers/systemd/ubi8.container
[Container]
Exec=id
Image=ubi8
#PodmanArgs=--group-add keep-groups
GroupAdd=keep-groups
UserNS=keep-id
```

Now as a systemd service
```
$ systemctl --user daemon-reload
$ systemctl --user start ubi8.service
$ systemctl --user status ubi8.service
...
Jan 08 11:24:52 ygal-linux systemd-ubi8[228072]: uid=1001(rootless_usr) gid=1002(rootless_usr) groups=1002(rootless_usr),65534(nobody)
...
```

I'm running on Fedora 42, but the same Podman version `5.7.1`. So, I'm not sure why it's not working as you expected for you
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I can confirm I have the same issue on Debian 13 (trixie).
`sudo -u jellyfin podman run  --user 116:1002 --group-add=keep-groups ...` works as expected.

On the other hand, when it's a quadlet:

```
[Container]
 User=116:1002
 UserNS=keep-id
 GroupAdd=keep-groups
```

`sudo systemctl -M jellyfin@ --user start jellyfin`

Gives me ` System.IO.IOException: Permission denied` when trying to modify a directory where only the group permissions match.

@Gremious can you provide a step-by-step to what you are doing, what you expect and what's failing? E.g. the complete Quadlet file, the directory definitions, the user definition, etc.
BTW have you tried logging in as `jellyfin` and then starting the service (instead of using `sudo` and `-M`)?

> BTW have you tried logging in as jellyfin and then starting the service (instead of using sudo and -M)?

Interesting idea, I tried that now, but still doesn't work unfortunately.

---
> can you provide a step-by-step to what you are doing, what you expect and what's failing? E.g. the complete Quadlet file, the directory definitions, the user definition, etc.

I just spun up a fresh VM of Debian 13.5.0 and re-did it from scratch. The issue still remained so here's every step.
Apologies, I'm a bit lazy today, so it's not formatted the best, but it should work fine. Hopefully I didn't miss anything.

sudo apt install podman
sudo adduser --home /home/jellyfin jellyfin 
sudo loginctl enable-linger jellyfin
sudo -u jellyfin mkdir -p /home/jellyfin/.config/containers/systemd
sudo -u jellyfin touch /home/jellyfin/.config/containers/systemd/jellyfin.network 
sudo -u jellyfin nvim /home/jellyfin/.config/containers/systemd/jellyfin.network 

> Then write the single line [Network] in it.

sudo -u jellyfin touch /home/jellyfin/.config/containers/systemd/jellyfin.container
sudo -E nvim /home/jellyfin/.config/containers/systemd/jellyfin.container


> Paste this:

```
[Unit]
Description=jellyfin on my.domain
After=network-online.target

[Container]
Image=docker.io/jellyfin/jellyfin:latest
ContainerName=jellyfin
User=1001:1002
GroupAdd=keep-groups
UserNS=keep-id
# You can play around with these values, no variation seem to work
# UserNS=keep-id:uid=1001,gid=1002
# Annotation="run.crun.keep_original_groups=1"

Network=jellyfin.network
PublishPort=8096:8096/tcp
PublishPort=7359:7359/udp
Volume=/home/jellyfin/.config/jellyfin:/config
Volume=/home/jellyfin/.cache/jellyfin:/cache
Volume=/data/jellyfin:/media
# EnvironmentFile=/home/jellyfin/env

[Service]
Restart=unless-stopped

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

```

sudo groupadd --gid 1002 hdd_data

sudo -u jellyfin mkdir -p /home/jellyfin/.cache/jellyfin
sudo -u jellyfin mkdir -p /home/jellyfin/.config/jellyfin

sudo mkdir -p /data/jellyfin/movies/bbb
sudo chown -R 1000:1002 /data
wget --directory-prefix /data/jellyfin/movies/bbb https://download.blender.org/peach/bigbuckbunny_movies/BigBuckBunny_320x180.mp4
sudo chown -R 1000:1002 /data
sudo chmod -R g+w /data/

sudo usermod --append --groups systemd-journal,hdd_data jellyfin
sudo systemctl -M jellyfin@ --user daemon-reload

sudo systemctl -M jellyfin@ --user start jellyfin

> In terminal

sudo -u jellyfin journalctl --user -xfu jellyfin

> In browser

http://localhost:8096

Go through start flow
Add movie library pointing at /media/movies
Scroll down and enable "Nfo" data.
Go to movies, bbb, right click, refresh metadata (last option, might need to scroll the pop-out menu), replace all existing metadata

See in the journalctl terminal the following:

> Jun 06 17:50:26 minimal-vm-debian jellyfin[4696]: System.UnauthorizedAccessException: Access to the path '/media/movies/bbb/movie.nfo' is denied.

And yet, this is running as:
1. "User=1001:1002"
2. `GroupAdd=keep-groups` + `UserNS=keep-id`
3. Where we `sudo chown -R 1000:1002 /data`
4.  and `sudo chmod -R g+w /data/` 
so it should be able to access it via group 1002.

---

> for fun and more info:

sudo apt install jq
sudo -u jellyfin podman inspect --format json jellyfin | jq '.[0].State.Pid'
sudo nsenter -a -t <the_id_from_above> -n /bin/bash

id jellyfin
getent group 1002
(You pinged me so maybe you want to be pinged for notifs, so here you go just in case @ygalblum )
I can confirm I have the same issue with GroupAdd not working for rootless podman quadlets on Fedora 44 CoreOS.
